ops.title: Any script/config duplicated across fleet repos must be a cc-guides-managed artifact, not per-repo copies
ops.body:
User rule (2026-07-19, re captain-hook helper scripts): when a script or artifact is the same (or same-shaped) across fleet repos — version-xcconfig generators, appicon resizers, CI stubs — it must be a cc-guides-managed artifact (shared fragment in cc-skills, rendered per-repo via `.claude/fragments/<target>/layout.toml` + `cc-guides render`), never hand-copied per-repo files. Repo-specific scripts are fine in-tree. Apply this mindset to everything across all repos.

**Why:** hand-copies drift; cc-guides render keeps one source of truth with per-repo composition.

**How to apply:** before committing a new script, check sibling repos for an equivalent; on a match, lift it into a cc-skills fragment and wire both layout dirs instead of committing a copy. When shipping, sweep new files for fleet duplicates and file consolidation work if it can't run now. Related: [[no-loose-contract-doc-dirs]].
